body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Wire base_sha input into changed-test selection**

The workflow advertises a `workflow_dispatch` `base_sha` input, but the value is never consumed when building the test matrix, so manual runs cannot actually diff against the requested base commit. In `flake-go`, the selector only runs `whichtests --repo-root . --github-actions --coalesce` and never passes `base_sha`, which means dispatches intended to reproduce flakes against a specific base can silently select the wrong tests (or no tests) based on the tool's default base.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+name: flake-go
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      base_sha:
+        description: "Base commit to diff against. Defaults to merge-base against origin/main."
+        required: false
+        type: string

body:
The --github-actions mode in whichtests reads workflow_dispatch.inputs.base_sha and inputs.head_sha directly out of $GITHUB_EVENT_PATH, which GitHub Actions populates from the declared workflow_dispatch.inputs block. There is no separate CLI flag to thread; passing one would conflict with the event-driven contract.

See [githubactions.go in coder/whichtests](https://github.com/coder/whichtests/blob/ec33bab1ec04cd86beb7a61a069db4463dba63f5/githubactions.go) — the workflow_dispatch branch validates event.Inputs.BaseSHA, sets req.Range.BaseSHA, and adds an origin fetch for that SHA before planning. A dispatch with base_sha: deadbeef does diff against that commit.
diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+name: flake-go
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      base_sha:
+        description: "Base commit to diff against. Defaults to merge-base against origin/main."
+        required: false
+        type: string

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Include failure() in report condition**

When the flake check step actually fails, this report step will be skipped because GitHub Actions implicitly applies `success()` to any step `if` that does not contain a status check function. The CI workflow report steps use `failure() && ...`, but this new flake workflow only checks `steps.flake_check.outcome`, so the failure artifact and summary will not be produced for the exact test failures this workflow is meant to diagnose. Add `failure()` or `always()` to this condition before checking the step outcome.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,82 @@
+name: flake-go
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      base_sha:
+        description: "Base commit to diff against. Defaults to merge-base against origin/main."
+        required: false
+        type: string
+      head_sha:
+        description: "Head commit to analyze. Defaults to the checked out HEAD."
+        required: false
+        type: string
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+concurrency:
+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: true
+
+jobs:
+  flake_go:
+    name: Flake Check
+    runs-on: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'coder' && 'depot-ubuntu-22.04-4' || 'ubuntu-latest' }}
+    timeout-minutes: 20
+    steps:
+      - name: Harden Runner
+        uses: step-security/harden-runner@f808768d1510423e83855289c910610ca9b43176 # v2.17.0
+        with:
+          egress-policy: audit
+
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
+        with:
+          repository: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name || github.repository }}
+          ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.event.inputs.head_sha || github.sha }}
+          fetch-depth: 0
+          persist-credentials: false
+
+      - name: Setup Go
+        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-go
+
+      - name: Install whichtests
+        shell: bash
+        run: ./.github/scripts/retry.sh -- go install github.com/coder/whichtests@ec33bab1ec04cd86beb7a61a069db4463dba63f5
+
+      - name: Select changed tests
+        id: selector
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          whichtests \
+            --repo-root . \
+            --github-actions \
+            --coalesce \
+            --out-matrix "$RUNNER_TEMP/flake-matrix.json"
+
+      - name: Setup Terraform
+        if: ${{ fromJSON(steps.selector.outputs.matrix).include[0] != null }}
+        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-tf
+
+      - name: Run targeted Go flake checks
+        id: flake_check
+        if: ${{ fromJSON(steps.selector.outputs.matrix).include[0] != null }}
+        uses: ./.github/actions/test-go-pg
+        with:
+          postgres-version: "13"
+          test-parallelism-packages: "4"
+          test-parallelism-tests: "16"
+          test-count: "25"
+          test-packages: ${{ fromJSON(steps.selector.outputs.matrix).include[0].package }}
+          run-regex: ${{ fromJSON(steps.selector.outputs.matrix).include[0].run_regex }}
+          test-shuffle: "on"
+          gotestsum-json-file: default
+
+      - name: Publish Go test failure report
+        if: ${{ steps.flake_check.outcome == 'failure' && github.actor != 'dependabot[bot]' && (github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork) }}
